item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved it more and more as the article went on, the ending was great!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a really solid SCP but not super great or anything. Still would’ve +1’ed, but was between that and neutral.
… but then you went and hit me in the feels. omg this article went from good to great with just that one line! Absolutely love this SCP! You deserve some kind of award ;P
EDIT: I just noticed that it only gave itself a bronze trophy!!! Poor 2900

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Poor trophy is sad that it cant make anyone happy… Proably one of the nicest scips we got!
take my plus one and tell that trophy it made me happy
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always love an article that surprises me with pity for the skip itself!

Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the one with agent yadav a reference to something? Otherwise, I absolutely love this article! It's one of my favorites. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you SCP-2900, you did a good job of making us happy!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a sucker for these kinds of stories. There's a dash of creepiness, but it's generally just delightful, and that's a breath of fresh air. There's possibly a consistency issue I'd like to point out, though: the article mentions that "[h]undreds of instances" were found at the school, but the first instance awarded to someone at the facility is 136. There seems to be a disparity in the numbers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is amazing. It's like the spirit of participation awards made manifest, an SCP that just wants to make people happy by congratulating them for mundane things. It really made me feel for the SCP at the end when it ironically awarded itself for an "attempt at making people happy". +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I the only one who was reminded of SCP-871? This is kind of like that, but with awards and trophies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, because it made me :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for "Best female janitor under 160 pounds!" because that's so much worse than a Participant award.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really loved this one! +1
Just wanted to see if I understood something well, was Doctor Yang the only one to be given a gold trophy because he was the one who edited the containment procedure so that the recipients could keep their instances? Because if so I really really love this one!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shouldn't the awards be called 2900-1's?

Other than that, good read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't expecting you to punch me right in the feels.
Take your upvote.

Type of Accolade Accompanying Text Recipient
An Upvote Latest article to reach +100! SCP-2900

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My daughter loves those movies, so that's exactly where my mind went, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Type of Accolade Accompanying Text Recipient
Gold medal with blue ribbon You made your first SCP article! Pixeltasim

(uh-oh. I think it got out)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bit of a late reply, but: it's not easily contained, and can spread easily given a lapse in security. That's why. Nothing to do with danger or understanding, I assume.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What makes this Euclid? It's a thing that gives people trophies. It isn't hurting anything, and it seems pretty well understood.

item.title: I love this so much...
item.content:encoded:
…if I could, I'd upvote it twice!

So cute! So sad! So inventive! I didn't like it at first, but it got better and better. What a fantastic first!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…or forgotten.

Sorry, that was my first instinct on seeing the name of the article.

I like it. Almost seems like those achievements in games you get for, you know, jumping a few times or completing the first level.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a really great idea. And I like implicit implication that this SCP is responsible for the plethora of participation awards. I'd be curious what other sorts of awards might have been given out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an oddball. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice one. Could still use a little more tinkering (especially the aforementioned strikethroughs on the containment protocols) but I love the idea. Agree with the previous poster, admit I feel a little sad for the poor thing. The "you did your best" one was when I decided I was going to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely love the idea of real life, "achievement unlocked" situations. Not to alter the idea of this entire article, but I wish there were a way for this thing to be completely safe; thousands of badges and trophies appearing in a day would obviously be a huge problem, but I'd just love to see this as a completely harmless 'for fun' SCP.
Good ending, too. It's the only time I had the feels for a skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea of irl achievements, but the increasing range-of-effect is such a stupid cliché. In addition, the containment procedures are pretty lax on actual containment, and also don't make much sense and don't fit with the ending. I also didn't really like the 163th incident.

However, none of that matters in the end, because the ending is brilliant. Achievement-granting anomaly giving itself achievement for trying its best at giving everyone achievements is the best thing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my feelings.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh, I thought this would be Nobody-related article, but I was wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a suggestion, but I think you should strikethrough the outdated Special Containment Procedures and remove the capitalized update notice. In its current format it doesn't read as especially Foundation-esque.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea isn't particularly novel, but it's decently written except for the hellishly confusing first paragraph. You say that SCP-2900 is a phenomenon… and then SCP-2900 instances are awards. You're contradicting yourself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just loved this. Goofy and heartwarming while still being believable.
A well-deserved +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad you enjoyed it! And thanks for the edits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to hug an anomalous trophy-providing effect? How did you make me do that?

Oh, and I edited a bit for punctuation. Forgot to give an edit reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Saw this in chat, and the more I think about it, the more I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ending is sort of dumb, but I upvoted for this:

You haven't hurt anyone in 24 hours!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Strangely enough this is my first SCP. Thanks to everyone in chat for feedback!
